entries.title: OpenAI Expands Daybreak Cybersecurity Initiative, Releases Full GPT-5.5-Cyber
entries.summary: On June 22–23, OpenAI expanded its Daybreak platform — releasing GPT-5.5-Cyber (85.6% on CyberGym benchmark), the Patch the Planet open-source remediation program, and a new Cyber Partner Program integrating with IBM, Cisco, CrowdStrike and others — intensifying the AI-driven cybersecurity arms race against Anthropic's Glasswing/Mythos and raising dual-use governance questions OpenAI says it addresses via tiered access controls and CAISI pre-deployment coordination.

links.title: SecurityWeek: OpenAI refocuses on patching over discovery
Cybernews: Experts warn AI finds flaws faster than defenders can fix

entries.title: FERC Unanimously Orders 6 US Grid Operators to Justify or Reform AI Data Center Power Rules Within 60 Days
entries.summary: On June 18, FERC issued tailored show-cause orders to PJM, MISO, Southwest Power Pool, CAISO, ISO-NE, and NYISO — covering roughly 200 million Americans — directing each to defend or rewrite tariffs for large-load customers within 60 days, marking the first binding federal regulatory action to treat AI infrastructure power demand as a national priority.

links.title: Data Center Knowledge: FERC targets grid rules for data centers

entries.title: EU Selects EUROPA Consortium to Build Open-Source 400B+ Parameter Frontier AI Model in All 24 EU Languages
entries.summary: On June 19, the European Commission chose the Domyn-led EUROPA consortium (with Fraunhofer-Gesellschaft) as winner of its Frontier AI Grand Challenge, awarding 2.5% of EuroHPC annual compute to train an open-source, 400B+ parameter model in all 24 official EU languages — the EU's clearest move to compete at the model layer rather than just regulate, raising open-weight governance and strategic-autonomy questions.

entries.title: OpenAI- and Anthropic-Backed Super PACs Spend $43M+ in 2026 Midterms, Waging Rival AI Regulation Wars
entries.summary: NPR reported June 22–24 that OpenAI-aligned 'Leading the Future' (funded by a16z and Greg Brockman, ~$23.5M spent) and Anthropic-backed 'Public First' (~$16.6M+) are running opposing campaigns in dozens of congressional races — OpenAI pushing deregulation, Anthropic opposing federal preemption of state AI safeguards — marking a new era of direct frontier-lab electoral intervention in AI governance.

links.title: NPR: Groups tied to OpenAI and Anthropic spending big on midterms

entries.title: China Retaliates Against 56 US Firms with Export Controls and Procurement Bans After Pentagon AI-Related Blacklist
entries.summary: On June 22, China's Ministry of Commerce placed 10 US companies (including rare-earth miners MP Materials and USA Rare Earth) on its export control list and banned 46 others from Chinese government procurement, directly retaliating for the Pentagon's June 8 expansion of its military-linked blacklist (adding Alibaba, Baidu, BYD) — escalating US–China tech bifurcation with direct implications for AI chip and critical-minerals supply chains.

links.title: CNBC: China imposes trade curbs on dozens of US firms
Al Jazeera: China adds 10 US firms to export control list

entries.title: 'Pickle in the Middle' — Unit 42 Discloses Bucket-Squatting Flaw Enabling Cross-Tenant RCE via Vertex AI Model Poisoning
entries.summary: Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 publicly disclosed (June 16) that Google's Vertex AI Python SDK (versions 1.139–1.140) used a predictable, ownership-unverified staging bucket name, letting an attacker with no victim access pre-register the bucket and swap model artifacts mid-upload — achieving remote code execution via pickle deserialization in Vertex AI's serving infrastructure; the bug is patched in v1.148.0 with no observed exploitation.

entries.title: EU AI Act Advisory Forum Holds Inaugural Session; AI Office Opens Call for GPAI External Evaluators
entries.summary: On June 19, the European Commission held the inaugural meeting of the AI Act Advisory Forum — the broad multi-stakeholder body advising on AI Act implementation — and the EU AI Office simultaneously published a call for participants in a July 15 workshop on qualification requirements for external evaluators of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk, marking the first concrete steps to operationalize third-party GPAI model evaluation under EU law.

links.title: EU AI Office (official, see press items for June 19)
EU AI Act standardisation page (Advisory Forum detail)

entries.title: EU Consultation on High-Risk AI Classification Guidelines Closes June 23; Final Guidelines Expected Ahead of August 2 Deadline
entries.summary: The European Commission's targeted stakeholder consultation on draft guidelines for classifying high-risk AI systems closed June 23, with feedback to be incorporated before official adoption — the finalized guidelines must be ready ahead of the EU AI Act's August 2, 2026 full-applicability date, creating an extremely compressed timeline for businesses in high-risk sectors.

entries.title: arXiv 2605.24383 — Open-Weight AI Model License Restrictions Lose Traceability After ~7 Downstream Generations ('Governance Horizon')
entries.summary: Auditing 2.1 million Hugging Face repositories, this paper (submitted May 23) finds that restriction evidence in open-weight model lineages decays with a half-life of 1.31 derivation steps, and beyond 7 downstream generations at least 80% of models lack sufficient public evidence for a governance determination — a 'governance horizon' showing that disclosure-based policies alone cannot sustain ethical-use traceability at scale.
